title: Videoflow Component Descriptor
description: Describes one language-agnostic component so videoflow can wire it into a graph, deploy it, and validate its use — without importing any of its code. One component.yaml per component (PROTOCOL.md §, migration plan Phase 2).

protocol.description: Protocol major version the component implements (spec/PROTOCOL.md).

finite.description: Producers only: whether next() self-terminates (Job vs Deployment). Default true.

cpu.description: OCI image ref for CPU deployment.

gpu.description: OCI image ref for GPU deployment.

pythonClass.description: Fully-qualified Python class the component provides, e.g. 'videoflow_contrib.detector_tf.TensorflowObjectDetector'. Present ⇒ this is a Python component run by 'python -m videoflow.worker' (the image must be videoflow-base-derived). Absent ⇒ a native component that runs its own image entrypoint/command and must speak the protobuf wire.

command.description: Container command override for a native component; default is the image entrypoint.

localCommand.description: Command to run a native component directly (no docker) under the local engine.

schema.description: JSON Schema for the component's constructor params (delivered via VF_NODE_PARAMS_JSON).

missing.description: Tolerates a None input (quorum join). Default false.

collected.description: Tolerates a list input (collect join). Default false.

partitionable.description: May be replicated with partition_by. Default false.

singleton.description: Must run as a single task (nb_tasks forced to 1). Default false.
